briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their AI-powered shopping app was discovered to actually be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware provider Synapse, raising urgent questions about consumer protections in embedded banking.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a sovereign domestic payment network, reducing reliance on US card giants Visa and Mastercard.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competitive dynamics in the payments industry.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of open banking firm Credit Kudos signals the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial data and consumer credit services.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Osler Deal Points Report finds Canada's venture recovery was uneven in 2025](https://betakit.com/osler-deal-points-report-finds-canadas-venture-recovery-was-uneven-in-2025/)** — *BetaKit* — Osler's latest report reveals that while Canada's venture capital market showed signs of recovery in 2025, gains were concentrated and not felt evenly across sectors or regions.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's federal government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and committed to an open banking framework, a milestone relevant to where Canadian implementation stands today.

- **[Ballooning AI costs have Canadian startups weighing alternatives](https://betakit.com/ballooning-ai-costs-have-canadian-startups-weighing-alternatives/)** — *BetaKit* — Rising AI infrastructure costs are forcing Canadian startups, including fintechs, to explore more cost-effective alternatives to dominant AI platforms.

- **[Grok's sexual deepfakes violated Canadian privacy law, says privacy commissioner](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/grok-deepfakes-privacy-commissioner-9.7231471?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's Privacy Commissioner ruled that Elon Musk's Grok AI violated Canadian privacy law by generating non-consensual sexual deepfakes, spotlighting growing regulatory scrutiny of AI platforms.

- **[Maneva raises $27-million USD Series A to expand AI vision in manufacturing](https://betakit.com/maneva-raises-27-million-usd-series-a-to-expand-ai-vision-in-manufacturing/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian AI startup Maneva secured $27M USD in Series A funding to scale its computer vision technology in manufacturing, reflecting continued investor appetite for Canadian tech ventures.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — This critical resource catalogues the darker consequences of fintech growth — including account freezes, opaque algorithms, and consumer harm — offering important context for building a responsible, inclusive fintech ecosystem in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Osler Deal Points Report finds Canada's venture recovery was uneven in 2025](https://betakit.com/osler-deal-points-report-finds-canadas-venture-recovery-was-uneven-in-2025/)** — *BetaKit* — The uneven nature of Canada's VC recovery underscores the importance of dedicated ecosystem-building efforts in regions like Atlantic Canada, where access to capital continues to lag behind central Canada.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — As Canada's open banking framework continues to evolve, this analysis of how open banking drives payments competition is directly relevant to credit unions and fintech startups across the Atlantic provinces preparing for implementation.
